channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Iran executes over 1K prisoners in 2024, highest total in 30 years, report says
item.description: A new report claimed Iran executed an unprecedented number of people in 2024. Most of the executions took place following the election of its new president in late July.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ISIS increasingly unopposed following US withdrawal from Afghanistan, collapse of Syria
item.description: The Islamic State is seeing less resistance in areas of major security concern following the U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan, the fall of the Assad regime in Syria and potential troop drawdown in Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's strength and unpredictability can help end the war with Russia, Ukraine's president says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says U.S. President-elect Donald Trump is “strong and unpredictable” and those qualities can be a decisive factor in his policy approach to Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza as stalled ceasefire talks set to resume
item.description: Hospital staff say at least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and Friday morning, as air sirens sounded across Israel and stalled ceasefire talks were set to resume.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Sam Mednick, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea's impeached president defies warrant after hourslong standoff
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators left the official residence of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after a near-six-hour standoff on Friday during which he defied their attempt to detain him.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: German and French foreign ministers seek 'new beginning' with Syria
item.description: The German and French foreign ministers are heading to Damascus to send what the German minister said is a clear signal that a “political new beginning” between Europe and Syria is possible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korean investigators attempt to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean investigators arrived at the presidential residence with a warrant to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived imposition of martial law, but faced resistance from presidential security staff as hundreds of Yoon’s supporters gathered outsid
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israeli airstrike hits a Gaza humanitarian zone as Netanyahu OKs a delegation to talks in Qatar
item.description: Israeli airstrikes across Gaza have killed at least 50 people, including several children, as they struck Hamas security officers and an Israeli-declared humanitarian zone.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa And Fatma Khaled, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Puerto Rico's new governor sworn in days after a major blackout left much of the island in the dark
item.description: Jenniffer González Colón has been sworn in as Puerto Rico’s new governor as the island held for a normally ebullient ceremony overshadowed by widespread anger over a blackout that hit the U.S. territory days ago.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Montenegro mourns after gunman kills at least 12 people before shooting himself
item.description: Shock and dismay prevails in Montenegro after a gunman fatally shot 12 people, including two children, in a western town before killing himself.
item.dc:creator: Predrag Milic, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: An armed man kills at least 10 people, including 2 children, in a shooting rampage in Montenegro
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — At least 10 people, including two children, were killed and four others were seriously wounded on Wednesday in a shooting rampage that followed a bar brawl in a western Montenegrin city, officials said. The shooter was on the run.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Gaza, including children, as war grinds into the new year
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes killed at least 12 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip, mostly women and children, officials said Wednesday, as the nearly 15-month war ground on into the new year.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — From Sydney to Mumbai to Paris to Rio de Janeiro, communities around the world welcomed 2025 with spectacular light shows, embraces and ice plunges.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, CEDAR ATTANASIO and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine stops transport of Russian gas to Europe
item.description: Ukraine has halted the transportation of Russian gas supplies through the country after a prewar transit deal expired at the end of last year, the nation's energy minister confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: Migrants accused of killing Mexican border agent after he asked for their IDs: officials
item.description: Three suspected migrants were arrested Monday for allegedly killing a Mexican immigration agent who had asked to see their identification documents, Mexican officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Airline says pre-flight inspection of South Korea plane showed 'no issues': report
item.description: Investigators are in South Korea searching through plane crash debris to try to determine what caused the country's deadliest air disaster in decades, which killed 179.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Dozens of patients and wounded evacuated from Gaza for treatment
item.description: Dozens of patients and the wounded have been evacuated for treatment outside the war-ravaged Gaza Strip, where the United Nations says Israel's attacks on and around hospitals have pushed health care to the brink.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Berlin police say they have detained a man who attacked 2 people with a knife
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Berlin police said they detained a man Tuesday who attacked and injured two people in the city's Charlottenburg neighborhood, further jolting Germany days after a deadly Christmas market attack elsewhere in the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US levels sanctions on entities in Iran, Russia over attempted election interference
item.description: The Biden administration announced new sanctions against Russia and Iran on Tuesday in response to efforts by both countries to influence the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Lemon calls Trump supporters 'f‑‑‑ing idiots' amid H-1B visa infighting
item.description: Former CNN host Don Lemon poked fun at the Republican Party's infighting over H-1B visas — a key program to attract international talent that’s been criticized as too complicated and susceptible to abuse — calling supporters of President-elect Trump "dumb f‑‑‑ing idiots.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Running a marathon a day in 2024, she has a world record within her grasp
item.description: GHENT, Belgium (AP) — Finally coming to a halt on Tuesday — the last day of 2024 — Belgian ultra runner Hilde Dosogne felt she had done all to take the world record as the first woman to run a marathon every single day of the year.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu goes against doctor’s orders, appears in Israeli parliament after surgery
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu appeared in the Israeli parliament on Tuesday for a crucial budget-related vote, two days after prostate surgery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zimbabwe's new currency woes hit traditional stores while illegal night bazaars flourish
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — Batsirai Pabwe picked detergents, toothpaste, snacks and some pasta among several grocery items spread on the tarmac of a car parking space — an unorthodox night bazaar lit by cellphone flashlights and fluorescent lamps in Zimbabwe’s capital, Harare.
item.dc:creator: FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arrest warrant issued for impeached South Korean president as political crisis deepens
item.description: A South Korean court approved arrest warrants on Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol and search his office and residence amid a criminal investigation into his martial law decree.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean court issues warrants to detain impeached President Yoon and search his office
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court issued warrants Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol and search his office and residence over allegations of rebellion in connection with his short-lived declaration of martial law.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Uncovering the atrocities of the Assad regime at its 'death factory' on the hill
item.description: Sednaya prison was the site where thousands of Syrians were tortured and killed under Assad’s regime. Investigators have uncovered mass graves, and are calling for justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
